name: Wolfe et al. Simulated Essay Prompt Strategy Extract
population_or_context: Simulated student essays for automated-scoring data augmentation.
sample_size: 96 real student essays and 288 simulated essays generated across three prompt-engineering strategies.
methods: Extracted the three prompt strategies and their role in producing simulated essays from real student essays.
education_context: Student essays scored with a four-point rubric.
response_origin: LLM-generated simulated essays derived from real student essays.
label_source: Expert raters assign scores and realism judgments to simulated essays; original essays had professional scores.
generation_model: Large language model; the public preprint text reviewed here does not name a specific model version.
generation_method: Prompt-based generation of simulated essays from real student essays.
prompt_strategy: 25 examples, Predict Next, and Sentence prompting strategies are compared.
evaluation_method: Expert scoring, score-distribution comparison, agreement metrics, and realism ratings.
scorer_use: Data augmentation calibration for automated scoring.
real_response_comparator: Real student essays are the basis and comparator for simulated essay generation.
reported_metrics: 96 real essays, 288 simulated essays, exact agreement, weighted kappa, score-distribution comparisons, and realism ratings.
limitations: The public extract does not name a specific model version.
The generation methods are essay-specific and prompt-strategy-specific.
